source.name: How to Respond to the 2026-2027 Threat Landscape

methodology_notes: Synthesized from Gartner CISO Survey 2026 + IBM X-Force Threat Intelligence Index 2026 + Microsoft / VulnCheck threat data + industry incident analysis. Aggregate findings extracted from published Gartner research note G00852689 for internal calibration reference. License confirmation outstanding before redistribution; current use is reference-distribution-only inside Principe's statistical model. Track per Product Council follow-up.

distributions.context: 53% of organizations reported a cybersecurity budget increase of 10% or less in 2026 (2026 Gartner CISO Survey)
48% of surveyed organizations put investment to address phishing in their top three threats; 37% included vulnerability exploitation management
41% of organizations report having experienced a deepfake + social engineering attack on an audio call to an employee
35% experienced deepfake + social engineering on a video call
Reported vulnerability volume grew 26.4% in Q1 2026 (vs. 20.4% in 2025 vs 2024). NIST reported a 33% increase year-over-year.
Only 32% of CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities were patched within Verizon's window. Inverse (~68%) implies post-breach exploitation likelihood for known KEVs.
VulnCheck reports 29% of KEVs were exploited before or on the day a CVE was published (up from 24% in 2024)
A single brand impersonation point was involved in more than 60% of all phishing attempts that Microsoft observed
IBM X-Force 2026 shows a 44% increase in incidents caused by exploits as initial access vector
Threat actors specifically targeting cloud environments rose 37% in 2025
Ransomware groups targeting industrial organizations increased 49% year-over-year
Attacks against core industrial protocols rose 84% in 2025
Unique internet-exposed CPS (cyber-physical system) assets jumped by 332%; nearly 20 million devices exposed
Approximately 16% of employees' prompts to AI included potentially sensitive content
Analysis shows a relative 32% increase of indirect prompt injections between observation periods
Some groups exploit vulnerabilities before public disclosure in 67% of observed cases
